data.title: Rio Tinto commissions $1.5 billion AP60 smelter expansion in Quebec
data.description: In Saguenay, Canada, Rio Tinto has initiated the commissioning process for its $1.5 billion AP60 smelter expansion at Complexe Arvida in Quebec. This signifies a significant advancement for the implementation of their advanced, eco-friendly aluminium smelting technology. The start of this expansion project commenced in March and is projected to conclude by the end of 2026, with all 96 new pots operational. This development is anticipated to enhance the plant’s production capacity by approximately 160,000 metric tonnes of primary aluminium annually.
RIO TINTO STARTS $1.5 BILLION AP60 SMELTER EXPANSION IN QUEBEC, EXPECTED TO FINISH BY END OF 2026.

data.title: Enterprise Application Support Teams Risk Falling Behind Cloud Delivery Without Skill Realignment, Says Info-Tech Research Group
data.description: Organizations are updating enterprise applications and transitioning from on-premises setups to cloud and hybrid environments. This change is causing challenges for traditional support models. The Info-Tech Research Group has recently released a blueprint titled "Right-Size Your Enterprise Application Support Team" to address this issue. The blueprint offers guidance on adapting support teams to the changing environment.

data.title: CrowdStrike Named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection for Seventh Consecutive Time
data.description: In Austin, Texas, cybersecurity company CrowdStrike (NASDAQ: CRWD) has been recognized as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Endpoint Protection for the seventh year in a row. They also ranked highest for Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute among all vendors evaluated for the fourth consecutive time. CrowdStrike is known for their AI-native endpoint security solutions and is leading the way in AI Detection and Response technologies. Their consistent innovation has solidified their position as a top player in the industry.

data.title: Tachyus to Join SLB to Accelerate AI- Driven Reservoir Management
data.description: Tachyus, known for its AI and Data Physics™ reservoir management technologies, has been acquired by SLB. This acquisition will integrate Tachyus' advanced modeling and optimization platforms with SLB's extensive global reach, digital ecosystem, and industry knowledge. The acquisition was finalized on May 29, 2026, in Houston.

data.title: Okta Stock Surges After Surprising Q1 Results, FY27 Boost
data.description: Shares of Okta Inc (NASDAQ: OKTA) surged as the company's first-quarter report exceeded expectations, with revenue hitting $765 million, surpassing the forecasted $751.88 million. Earnings per share stood at 91 cents, higher than the estimated 85 cents. The subscription backlog grew to $4.72 billion, a 16% increase from the previous year, while the cRPO rose 12% to almost $2.50 billion. Non-GAAP operating income was $191 million, 25% of total revenue, indicating significant operating leverage. Following this strong performance, price targets from BTIG and Needham were raised, fueling the market's positive response to the news. Okta stock is currently testing critical resistance levels, prompting interest in why the stock is breaking out. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: SpaceX's IPO frenzy driven by Elon Musk attracts Wall Street with promise of high returns, enticing investors to join.
data.description: Elon Musk and his team of financial experts are strategically planning to ensure the success of SpaceX’s upcoming initial public offering (IPO). Their goal is to cultivate a sense of urgency among potential investors, making them believe that the potential risks associated with not investing in the stock are greater than those of actually buying shares. This approach aims to generate significant interest and support for the IPO, with the intention of driving the company's valuation and market performance.
